questions.question: According to the law of exponents, a^m × a^n = :
questions.explanation: When multiplying like bases, add exponents: a^m × a^n = a^(m+n).

questions.question: Circumference of a circle of radius r is:
questions.explanation: The circumference formula is C = 2πr.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of its two:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle property: an exterior angle equals the sum of its two opposite (remote) interior angles.

questions.question: A triangle can have two right angles.
questions.explanation: If a triangle had two right angles, sum would exceed 180°, which is impossible.

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus are 10 cm and 8 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = (product of diagonals)/2 = (10×8)/2 = 40 cm².

questions.question: If 3 pens cost ₹45, what is the cost of 7 pens? (Assume direct proportion)
questions.explanation: Cost per pen = 45/3 = ₹15; cost of 7 pens = 7×15 = ₹105.

questions.question: What is the value of 2^5?

True or False: A square has 4 lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: two diagonals and two lines through midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.question: What is the area of a circle whose diameter is 14 cm? (Use π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Radius = 7 cm; Area = πr² = (22/7)×7×7 = 154 cm².

questions.question: In a triangle, the sum of the three interior angles is always
questions.explanation: Angle sum property of a triangle states that the sum of interior angles is 180°.

questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 12 × 5 = 60 sq cm.

questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent value. 3 appears three times.

questions.question: Two triangles are congruent by SSS rule if their corresponding sides are equal. (True/False)
questions.explanation: SSS (Side-Side-Side) congruence: if three sides of one triangle are equal to three sides of another, the triangles are congruent.

questions.question: The unit digit of 7^23 is
questions.explanation: Cyclic pattern of 7: 7^1=7, 7^2=49 (9), 7^3=343 (3), 7^4=2401 (1). 23 ÷ 4 remainder 3, so unit digit is 3.

questions.explanation: Area = (1/2) × (sum of parallel sides) × height = (1/2) × (8+12) × 5 = (1/2)×20×5 = 50 sq cm.

questions.question: If 4 pens cost ₹60, the cost of 10 pens (direct proportion) is
questions.explanation: Cost per pen = 60/4 = ₹15. For 10 pens: 15×10 = ₹150.

questions.question: Solve: 3x + 5 = 14. What is the value of x?

questions.explanation: Direct proportion: cost per chocolate = 160/8 = ₹20, so 12 chocolates cost 12×20 = ₹240.

questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles ⇒ 120° = 50° + other angle ⇒ other angle = 70°.

options: a^m / a^n = a^(m-n) for m > n

questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = ½ × d1 × d2 = ½ × 8 × 6 = 24 cm².

questions.explanation: Sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Third angle = 180° - (40°+60°) = 80°.

questions.question: Area of a parallelogram with base 12 cm and height 5 cm is:
questions.explanation: Area = base × height = 12 × 5 = 60 sq cm.

questions.question: The mode of the data 4,5,5,6,7,7,7,8 is:
questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent value. 7 occurs three times, more than any other.

questions.question: In a triangle, the exterior angle is 110° and one interior opposite angle is 60°. The other interior opposite angle is:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles. So 110° = 60° + other → other = 50°.

questions.explanation: All three statements are fundamental properties of triangles.

questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 15 × 8 = 120 sq cm.

questions.explanation: When a transversal cuts parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal.

questions.explanation: Law: a^m × a^n = a^(m+n).

questions.question: Which of the following conditions is sufficient to prove two triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: SSS (side-side-side) is a valid congruence criterion; AAA is not, SSA is ambiguous, ASA is also valid but SSS is given as one correct option.

questions.question: The degree of the expression 3x²y + 7xy² – 5 is
questions.explanation: Degree is highest sum of exponents in a term: x²y gives 2+1=3, xy² gives 1+2=3, constant has degree 0. So degree = 3.

questions.question: If x is directly proportional to y and x = 2 when y = 6, then the value of y when x = 5 is
questions.explanation: x ∝ y ⇒ x/y = constant. So 2/6 = 5/y ⇒ y = (5×6)/2 = 15.

questions.question: If 3x + 5 = 14, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides: 3x = 9, then divide by 3: x = 3.

questions.question: Which of the following are like terms? (a) 3x, 2y (b) 4ab, 7ab (c) 5p², 5q² (d) 2m, 2n
questions.explanation: Like terms have the same variable(s) raised to the same power. 4ab and 7ab are like terms.

questions.question: A parallelogram has base 12 cm and height 8 cm. Its area is
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 12 × 8 = 96 sq cm.

questions.question: The circumference of a circle with radius 7 cm is (take π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Circumference = 2πr = 2 × (22/7) × 7 = 44 cm.

questions.question: Area of a circle with radius 21 cm (π = 22/7) is
In a triangle, two angles are 45° and 65°. The third angle is
questions.explanation: Sum of angles = 180°. Third angle = 180° - (45°+65°) = 70°.

questions.question: If ΔABC ≅ ΔDEF, which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: Corresponding parts of congruent triangles are equal. So AC corresponds to DF.

questions.question: The arithmetic mean of the numbers 7, 9, 12, 15, 22 is
The mode of the data 3, 5, 6, 3, 7, 3, 8, 9 is
questions.explanation: The number 3 appears most frequently (3 times). So mode = 3.

questions.question: The median of the data 12, 15, 11, 19, 14, 10, 13 is
questions.explanation: Arrange in order: 10,11,12,13,14,15,19. Middle value (4th) is 13.

questions.question: If 5 meters of cloth costs ₹400, then the cost of 8 meters of cloth (direct proportion) is
questions.explanation: Cost per meter = 400/5 = ₹80. Cost of 8 m = 80 × 8 = ₹640.

questions.question: If 6 workers finish a job in 12 days, then 4 workers will finish the same job in (inverse proportion)
questions.explanation: Workers × days = constant: 6×12 = 72. Days for 4 workers = 72/4 = 18 days.

questions.question: In the expression 7x² - 3xy + 5, what is the coefficient of xy?
questions.explanation: The term -3xy has coefficient -3. The coefficient is the numerical factor attached to the variable part.

questions.question: If y varies directly as x and y = 15 when x = 3, find the constant of variation k.
questions.explanation: In direct proportion y = kx. Given y=15, x=3 gives k = y/x = 15/3 = 5.

questions.question: In any triangle, an exterior angle is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles.
questions.explanation: The exterior angle theorem states that the exterior angle equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles.

questions.question: Find the arithmetic mean of the data: 4, 8, 10, 12, 6.
questions.explanation: Sum = 4+8+10+12+6 = 40. Number of values = 5. Mean = 40/5 = 8.

questions.question: Simple Interest formula is I = P × N × R / 100. Find the simple interest on ₹5000 at 6% per annum for 3 years.
Two triangles are congruent if three sides of one triangle are equal to three sides of the other triangle. This condition is called _____.
questions.explanation: SSS (Side-Side-Side) congruence criterion: if all three sides are equal, triangles are congruent.

questions.question: If two angles are complementary and one is 40°, the other angle is
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum to 90°, so 90° − 40° = 50°.

questions.question: The number of diagonals in a quadrilateral is
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral has 2 diagonals.

questions.question: The mode of the data set {2, 3, 3, 5, 7, 7, 7, 8} is
questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent value; 7 occurs three times.

questions.question: In the identity (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b², if a=1 and b=2, the value of (a+b)² is
Simplify: (3^2)^3
questions.explanation: Using the law of exponents (a^m)^n = a^(m×n), we get (3^2)^3 = 3^(2×3) = 3^6.

questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 2:3:4. What is the measure of the largest angle?
questions.explanation: Sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Let the angles be 2x, 3x, 4x. Then 2x+3x+4x=9x=180, so x=20. Largest angle = 4x = 80°.

questions.question: Find the unit digit of 7^2023.
questions.explanation: Cyclic pattern of 7: 7,9,3,1 (period 4). 2023 mod 4 = 3, so unit digit is 3.

questions.question: Which identity is used to factorise x^2 - 9?
questions.explanation: x^2 - 9 is a difference of squares, factorised using (a+b)(a-b).

questions.question: In a triangle, the angles are in the ratio 2:3:4. Find the smallest angle.
questions.explanation: Sum of angles = 180°, ratio sum = 2+3+4 = 9, so each part = 20°. Smallest = 2×20 = 40°.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and its interior opposite angles are in the ratio 2:3. Find the larger interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation: Let the angles be 2x and 3x. Exterior angle = sum of opposite interior angles = 5x = 110° => x = 22°. Larger angle = 3×22 = 66°.

questions.question: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, the interior angles on the same side are complementary.
questions.explanation: Interior angles on the same side of a transversal are supplementary (sum 180°), not complementary (sum 90°).

questions.question: The circumference of a circle is 44 cm. Find its area. (Take π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Circumference = 2πr = 44 => r = 44/(2×22/7) = 7 cm. Area = πr² = (22/7)×49 = 154 cm².

questions.question: A circular garden of radius 7 m has a path of width 1.4 m around it. Find the area of the path. (Take π = 22/7)
The area of a parallelogram is 84 cm² and its base is 12 cm. Find its height.
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height => 84 = 12 × height => height = 7 cm.

questions.question: The mean of 5 numbers is 8. If one number is removed, the mean becomes 7.5. Find the removed number.
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 5×8 = 40. Sum of remaining 4 numbers = 4×7.5 = 30. Removed number = 40 - 30 = 10.

questions.question: Find the median of the first seven prime numbers.
questions.explanation: First seven primes: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17. Median (4th term) = 7.

questions.question: A sum of money doubles itself in 10 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest per annum?
questions.explanation: Let principal = P, then amount = 2P, so interest = P. Simple interest: P = P×R×10/100 => R = 10%.

questions.question: If 8 men can complete a work in 15 days, how many men are needed to complete the same work in 12 days?
questions.explanation: Work = men × days constant = 8×15 = 120. Required men = 120/12 = 10.

questions.question: Find the degree of the expression 5x^3y^2 + 2xy - 3.
questions.explanation: Degree is the highest sum of exponents of variables. In 5x^3y^2, sum = 3+2 = 5, which is the highest.

questions.question: The solution of the equation 3(x + 2) - 2(x - 1) = 7 is
questions.explanation: 3x+6-2x+2=7 → x+8=7 → x=-1. (Term I Algebra, Simple linear equations)

questions.question: In the figure, if l ∥ m and a transversal cuts them, ∠1 = 110°. Then ∠2 is
questions.explanation: ∠1 and ∠2 are interior angles on the same side of transversal, sum = 180°, so ∠2 = 70°. (Term I Geometry, Transversal)

questions.question: The area of a trapezium whose parallel sides are 12 cm and 8 cm and height is 5 cm is
questions.explanation: Area = ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height = ½ × (12+8)×5 = ½×20×5 = 50 sq cm. (Term I Measurements, Trapezium)

questions.question: The median of the data 15, 12, 18, 10, 14, 16, 20 is
questions.explanation: Arrange in ascending order: 10,12,14,15,16,18,20. Median (4th value) = 15. (Term III Statistics, Median)

questions.question: The value of (−2)^3 × (−2)^4 is
questions.explanation: Using law of exponents: a^m × a^n = a^(m+n). (−2)^(3+4) = (−2)^7. (Term II Algebra, Laws of Exponents)

questions.question: In a triangle, the exterior angle at a vertex is 120° and one of the interior opposite angles is 45°. The other interior opposite angle is
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles. So 120° = 45° + x → x = 75°. (Term II Geometry, Exterior Angles)

questions.question: A circular path of width 2 m is built around a circular park of radius 14 m. The area of the path (in sq m) is
questions.explanation: Outer radius = 16 m. Area of path = π(16^2−14^2) = π(256−196)=60π ≈ 188.4 (using π=3.14 gives 188.4, closest 188.48 if π=22/7 gives 188.57). Textbook uses π=22/7: 60×22/7=1320/7=188.57, but option 188.48 is approximate. (Term II Measurements, Area of Pathways)

questions.question: The degree of the expression 5x^3y^2 + 7x^2y − 3xy + 2 is
questions.explanation: Degree is highest sum of exponents of variables in a term. First term: 3+2=5; second: 2+1=3; third: 1+1=2; constant:0. So degree=5. (Term II Algebra, Degree of Expression)

questions.question: Two triangles are congruent by SSS criterion. If sides of one triangle are 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm, then the sides of the other triangle are
questions.explanation: SSS congruence: all three sides must be equal. So the other triangle also has sides 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm. (Term II Geometry, Congruency of Triangles)

questions.question: To construct a circle of radius 3.5 cm, the distance between the compass needle and pencil should be
questions.explanation: Radius is the distance from centre to any point on circle, so compass opening = 3.5 cm. (Term III Geometry, Construction of circles)

questions.question: The area of a rhombus whose diagonals are 12 cm and 16 cm is
questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = ½ × product of diagonals = ½ × 12 × 16 = 96 sq cm. (Term I Measurements, Rhombus)

questions.question: If 8 workers can complete a job in 15 days, then the number of workers required to complete the same job in 10 days is
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: workers × days = constant. 8×15 = 120. For 10 days: workers = 120/10 = 12. (Term I Direct and Inverse Proportion, Inverse proportion)

questions.question: Factorise: x^2 − 9 using identity.
questions.explanation: x^2 − 9 = x^2 − 3^2 = (x+3)(x−3) using identity a^2−b^2 = (a+b)(a−b). (Term III Algebra, Factorisation using identities)

questions.question: If the sum of three consecutive integers is 72, the smallest integer is:
questions.explanation: Let integers be x, x+1, x+2. Then 3x+3=72 → 3x=69 → x=23 (Term I, Chapter 3 – Algebra).

questions.question: A rhombus has one diagonal of 12 cm and area 96 sq cm. The length of the other diagonal is:
questions.explanation: Area of rhombus = (d1 × d2)/2 → 96 = (12 × d2)/2 → d2 = 16 cm (Term I, Chapter 2 – Measurements).

questions.question: The circumference of a circle with diameter 28 cm is (take π = 22/7):
questions.explanation: Circumference = πd = (22/7) × 28 = 88 cm (Term II, Chapter 2 – Measurements).

questions.question: In a trapezium, the parallel sides are 10 cm and 14 cm, and the distance between them is 8 cm. Its area is:
questions.explanation: Area = (1/2) × (sum of parallel sides) × height = (1/2) × (10+14) × 8 = 96 sq cm (Term I, Chapter 2 – Measurements).

questions.question: True or False: The exterior angle of a triangle is always equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles.
questions.explanation: This is the Exterior Angle Property of a triangle (Term II, Chapter 4 – Geometry).

questions.question: If 8 workers can complete a job in 12 days, how many workers are needed to complete the same job in 6 days?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 8 × 12 = x × 6 → x = 16 (Term I, Chapter 4 – Direct and Inverse Proportion).

questions.question: The simple interest on ₹6000 for 3 years at 5% per annum is:
questions.explanation: SI = (P×R×T)/100 = (6000×5×3)/100 = ₹900 (Term III, Chapter 2 – Percentage and Simple Interest).

questions.question: A circle of radius 7 cm has an area of (take π = 22/7):
questions.explanation: Area = πr² = (22/7) × 7 × 7 = 154 sq cm (Term II, Chapter 2 – Measurements).

questions.question: If the area of a parallelogram is 84 cm² and its base is 12 cm, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height. So, height = Area / base = 84 / 12 = 7 cm.

questions.question: In a rhombus, if one diagonal is 10 cm and the area is 60 cm², what is the length of the other diagonal?
If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, the number of pairs of corresponding angles formed is:
questions.explanation: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, 4 pairs of corresponding angles are formed.

questions.question: The value of the expression 3a² – 2ab + b² when a = –1 and b = 2 is:
The solution of the equation 2x + 5 = 13 is:
If 4 workers can build a wall in 9 days, how many days will 6 workers take to build the same wall?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 4 × 9 = 6 × d => d = 36 / 6 = 6 days.

questions.question: The area of a circular pathway of width 2 m around a circular park of radius 10 m is (use π = 3.14):
questions.explanation: Outer radius = 12 m. Area of pathway = π(12² – 10²) = 3.14 × (144 – 100) = 3.14 × 44 = 138.16 m².

questions.question: The value of 2⁵ × 2³ ÷ 2⁴ is:
questions.explanation: Using laws of exponents: 2⁵ × 2³ ÷ 2⁴ = 2^(5+3-4) = 2⁴.

questions.question: The degree of the expression 4x³y – 2xy² + 7 is:
questions.explanation: Degree is the highest sum of exponents in a term: 4x³y has degree 3+1=4.

questions.question: If the mode of the data 4, 7, 9, 7, 11, 7, 13 is 7, then the median of the same data is:
questions.explanation: Arrange in order: 4, 7, 7, 7, 9, 11, 13. Median (middle value) = 7.

questions.question: The identity (a – b)² = a² – 2ab + b² is used to find the value of 99². Which of the following is correct?
The area of a parallelogram with base 15 cm and height 8 cm is equal to the area of a rhombus. If one diagonal of the rhombus is 20 cm, what is the length of the other diagonal?
questions.explanation: Area of parallelogram = base × height = 15 × 8 = 120 sq cm. Area of rhombus = (1/2) × d1 × d2 = 120. So (1/2) × 20 × d2 = 120 → 10 × d2 = 120 → d2 = 12 cm.

questions.question: A garden is in the shape of a trapezium with parallel sides 14 m and 10 m, and distance between them is 6 m. Find the area of the garden (in sq m).
questions.explanation: Area of trapezium = (1/2) × (sum of parallel sides) × height = (1/2) × (14 + 10) × 6 = (1/2) × 24 × 6 = 72 sq m.

questions.question: The circumference of a circle is 88 cm. What is its radius? (Take π = 22/7)
State True or False: The value of 2³ × 3² is 72.
questions.explanation: 2³ = 8, 3² = 9, product = 8 × 9 = 72. Hence true.

questions.question: A rectangular park is 40 m long and 30 m wide. A path of uniform width 2 m is built inside the park along its boundary. Find the area of the path.
questions.explanation: Area of outer rectangle = 40 × 30 = 1200 sq m. Inner rectangle length = 40 – 4 = 36 m, width = 30 – 4 = 26 m, area = 936 sq m. Path area = 1200 – 936 = 264 sq m.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an identity?
questions.explanation: An identity is true for all values of variables. (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b² is an algebraic identity. Others are equations true only for specific values.

questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and one of its interior opposite angles is 45°. What is the other interior opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles → 110° = 45° + other angle → other angle = 65°.

questions.question: Which of these represents a pair of supplementary angles?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles sum to 180°. 30° + 150° = 180°.

questions.question: If the mean of 4 numbers is 15 and three of them are 12, 16, and 14, find the fourth number.
questions.explanation: Mean = sum/4 = 15 → sum = 60. Sum of given three = 12 + 16 + 14 = 42. Fourth = 60 – 42 = 18.

questions.question: Solve for x: 3x/4 + 5 = 17. What is the value of x?

State whether the following is true or false: For any non-zero integer a, a^m ÷ a^n = a^(m+n).
questions.explanation: The correct law is a^m ÷ a^n = a^(m−n). Hence false.

questions.question: In a triangle, the exterior angle is 120° and one of the interior opposite angles is 70°. What is the other interior opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles ⇒ 120° = 70° + x ⇒ x = 50°.

questions.explanation: The highest sum of exponents: 5x^3 (degree 3), 2x^2y (degree 3), −7xy^2 (degree 3), constant (degree 0). Hence degree 3.

questions.question: The marks of five students are 45, 52, 48, 52, 55. The mode and median respectively are
questions.explanation: Mode = 52 (occurs twice). Ordered: 45,48,52,52,55, median = 52.

questions.question: A figure has rotational symmetry of order 3. Which of the following is true?
options: True: The figure looks the same after every 120° rotation.
False: The figure looks the same after every 90° rotation.
questions.explanation: Order 3 means angle of rotation = 360°/3 = 120°.

questions.question: Find the simple interest on Rs 5000 for 2 years at 8% per annum.

questions.explanation: Outer area = 20×15=300; inner length=20−4=16, inner width=15−4=11, inner area=176; path area=300−176=124 sq m.

questions.question: Which of the following conditions is NOT sufficient to prove two triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: AAA gives similarity, not congruence.

questions.question: If x = -2, y = 3, then the value of 2x² - 3xy + y² is
questions.explanation: Substitute x = -2, y = 3: 2(4) - 3(-2)(3) + 9 = 8 + 18 + 9 = 35. Option '35' is correct.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m and t is a transversal. If ∠1 = 70°, then ∠8 is
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal. ∠1 and ∠8 are alternate exterior angles? Actually, when lines are parallel, alternate exterior angles are equal. ∠1 = 70°, so ∠8 = 70°.

questions.question: The area of a trapezium with parallel sides 12 cm and 18 cm, and height 8 cm is
questions.explanation: Area = (1/2) × (sum of parallel sides) × height = (1/2) × (12+18) × 8 = (1/2)×30×8 = 120 cm².

questions.question: If 5 workers can paint a wall in 8 days, how many days will 10 workers take to paint the same wall?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: Workers × Days constant. 5×8 = 10 × d → d = 4 days.

questions.question: In ΔABC, AB = 5 cm, BC = 6 cm, AC = 7 cm. In ΔDEF, DE = 5 cm, EF = 6 cm, DF = 7 cm. The congruence rule applied is
questions.explanation: All three sides are equal: SSS congruence.

questions.question: Find the median of the data: 12, 7, 15, 21, 10, 8, 14.
questions.explanation: Arrange ascending: 7,8,10,12,14,15,21. Median is the 4th value = 12.

questions.question: Two concentric circles have radii 15 cm and 9 cm. A chord of the larger circle is tangent to the smaller circle. The length of the chord is
questions.explanation: Perpendicular from center to chord is radius of inner circle = 9 cm. Half chord length = √(15² - 9²) = √(225-81)=√144=12 cm. Full chord = 24 cm.

questions.question: Solve for x: (2x)/3 - 5 = x/2 + 1.
A rhombus has perimeter 40 cm and one diagonal 12 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: Side = 40/4 = 10 cm. Half-diagonal = 6 cm. Other half-diagonal = sqrt(10^2 - 6^2) = 8 cm. Other diagonal = 16 cm. Area = (1/2)*12*16 = 96 sq cm.

questions.question: The mean of 5 numbers is 12. If one number 8 is removed, what is the new mean?
questions.explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 5*12 = 60. Remove 8 => sum = 52. New mean = 52/4 = 13.

questions.question: 6 machines can produce 240 bottles in 4 hours. How many bottles can 10 machines produce in 6 hours?
questions.explanation: Rate: 6 machines × 4 hours = 24 machine-hours for 240 bottles => 1 machine-hour = 10 bottles. Then 10 × 6 = 60 machine-hours => 60 × 10 = 600 bottles.

questions.question: Expand (2x + 3y)^2 using algebraic identity.
Which congruence condition is used to prove that triangles ABD and ACD are congruent if AB = AC and AD is altitude in triangle ABC?
questions.explanation: Right angle at D (AD ⟂ BC), hypotenuse AB = AC, side AD common. Hence RHS congruence.

questions.question: A circular pond of radius 7 m has a path of width 2 m around it. Find the area of the path. (Use π = 22/7)
Find the mode of the data: 2, 3, 5, 3, 4, 5, 3, 2, 6, 3.
questions.explanation: Mode is the value that appears most frequently. 3 appears 4 times, others less.

questions.question: At a certain rate of simple interest, a sum of money becomes ₹6600 in 2 years. If the rate of interest is 10% per annum, what is the principal?
Find the degree of the expression: 3x^2 y + 4 x y^2 - 5x^3 + 7.
questions.explanation: Degrees of terms: 2+1=3 for first, 1+2=3 for second, 3 for third, 0 for constant. Highest is 3.

questions.question: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, then the sum of the interior angles on the same side of the transversal is 180°. (True/False)
questions.explanation: This is a property of parallel lines: co-interior angles are supplementary.

questions.question: The value of 3x²y + 2xy² - x²y + xy² when simplified is:
questions.explanation: Combine like terms: 3x²y - x²y = 2x²y; 2xy² + xy² = 3xy². So result is 2x²y + 3xy².

questions.question: In a rhombus, if one diagonal is 10 cm and the area is 60 cm², the length of the other diagonal is:
If the exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and its interior opposite angles are in the ratio 2:3, then the larger interior opposite angle is:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles. Let angles be 2x and 3x. Then 2x+3x=110 => 5x=110 => x=22. Larger angle = 3x = 66°.

questions.question: The value of (2³)² × 2⁴ ÷ 2⁵ is:
questions.explanation: (2³)² = 2⁶. Then 2⁶ × 2⁴ = 2¹⁰. 2¹⁰ ÷ 2⁵ = 2⁵.

questions.question: In a direct proportion, if x = 5 when y = 20, then the value of y when x = 8 is:
If 2x + 5 = 17, then the value of 3x - 4 is:
The mode of the data 3, 5, 7, 5, 9, 5, 7, 8, 5 is:
questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent value. 5 appears 4 times, which is the highest frequency.

questions.question: The number of lines of symmetry of a regular hexagon is:
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry (through opposite vertices and through midpoints of opposite sides).

questions.question: If 3(x+2) + 2(x-1) = 4(x+3) - 5, find the value of x.
Expand (2x+3y)² using suitable identity.
questions.explanation: (a+b)² = a²+2ab+b²; here a=2x, b=3y gives 4x²+12xy+9y².

questions.question: In a triangle, the exterior angle at a vertex is 125°. If one interior opposite angle is 55°, find the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles; 125° = 55° + x → x = 70°.

questions.question: Find the area of a parallelogram with base 15 cm and corresponding height 10 cm.
questions.explanation: Area = base × height = 15 × 10 = 150 sq cm.

questions.question: Find the circumference of a circle with radius 21 cm. (Use π = 22/7)
Find the area of a circle whose diameter is 28 m. (Use π = 22/7)
Find the mean of the numbers: 5, 8, 12, 15, 20.
questions.explanation: Sum = 5+8+12+15+20=60; number of terms=5; mean=60/5=12.

questions.question: Find the mode of the data: 2, 3, 4, 2, 5, 2, 6, 3, 8, 2.
questions.explanation: 2 appears 4 times, others less; mode is 2.

questions.question: If 8 workers can build a wall in 15 days, how many days will 12 workers take to build the same wall?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 8×15 = 12×d → d = 120/12 = 10 days.

questions.question: Find the rate of interest (per annum) if ₹3000 amounts to ₹3600 in 2 years under simple interest.
When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, the corresponding angles are always equal.
questions.explanation: By definition, when a transversal cuts parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal.

questions.question: Solve: 3x - 5 ≤ 10, where x is a natural number. How many natural numbers satisfy this inequality?
questions.explanation: 3x ≤ 15 → x ≤ 5. Natural numbers = {1,2,3,4,5} → 5 numbers.

questions.question: In a parallelogram, the base is 12 cm and the height is 7 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: Area of a parallelogram = base × height = 12 × 7 = 84 sq cm. (Term I, Chapter 2 - Measurements)

questions.question: If the adjacent angles of a rhombus are (2x+10)° and (3x+20)°, find x.
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles of a rhombus are supplementary: (2x+10)+(3x+20)=180 → 5x+30=180 → 5x=150 → x=30. (Term I, Ch 2)

questions.question: 15 workers can build a wall in 20 days. How many days will 25 workers take to build the same wall?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 15 × 20 = 25 × d → d = 300/25 = 12 days. (Term I, Ch 4 - Direct and Inverse Proportion)

questions.question: In the figure, two lines intersect. One angle is 85°. What is the measure of its vertically opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. (Term I, Ch 5 - Geometry: Intersecting Lines)

questions.question: A die is rolled once. What is the probability of getting a prime number?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers on a die: 2,3,5 → 3 outcomes out of 6 → 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.question: Find the value of 2⁵ ÷ 2³.
questions.explanation: 2⁵ ÷ 2³ = 2^(5-3) = 2² = 4. (Term II, Ch 3 - Exponents & Powers)

questions.question: What is the degree of the expression 4x²y + 3xy² - 5?
questions.explanation: Degree is highest sum of powers of variables in a term: 4x²y → 2+1=3. (Term II, Ch 3 - Degree of Expression)

questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is 120° and one of its interior opposite angles is 50°. Find the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of interior opposite angles → 120 = 50 + x → x = 70°. (Term II, Ch 4 - Exterior Angles)

questions.question: If a : b = 3 : 4 and b : c = 2 : 5, find a : c.
Find the mode of the data: 5, 3, 7, 3, 8, 3, 9, 5, 7.
questions.explanation: Mode is the most frequent value: 3 appears three times. (Term III, Ch 5 - Statistics)

questions.question: Factorise: x² + 7x + 12.
questions.explanation: x² + 7x + 12 = (x+3)(x+4) because sum=7, product=12. (Term III, Ch 3 - Algebra: Factorisation)

questions.question: Solve for x: 2(x - 3) + 4 = 3x - 1.
In the figure, if l ∥ m and t is a transversal, corresponding angles are (3x+10)° and (5x−20)°. Find x.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal → 3x+10 = 5x−20 → 10+20 = 5x−3x → 30 = 2x → x = 15

questions.question: The parallel sides of a trapezium are 15.5 cm and 10.5 cm. Its height is 8 cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: Area = ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height = ½ × (15.5 + 10.5) × 8 = ½ × 26 × 8 = 104 sq cm

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false: 8² × 4³ = 2¹¹.
questions.explanation: 8² = (2³)² = 2⁶, 4³ = (2²)³ = 2⁶, product = 2¹² ≠ 2¹¹. Hence false.

questions.question: If 5 workers can paint a house in 12 days, how many days will 8 workers take to paint the same house?
questions.explanation: Number of workers and days are in inverse proportion. 5 × 12 = 8 × d → d = 60/8 = 7.5 days

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct? (i) (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b² (ii) (a−b)² = a² − 2ab + b² (iii) (a+b)(a−b) = a² + b² (iv) (a+b)² = a² + b²
questions.explanation: (i) and (ii) are correct standard identities. (iii) is false: (a+b)(a−b)=a²−b². (iv) is false: (a+b)² = a²+2ab+b².

questions.question: Solve the inequation: −3x + 5 > 2.
questions.explanation: −3x + 5 > 2 → −3x > −3 → dividing by −3 flips the inequality → x < 1
